meta.homeTitle: Freshman AI - Active Recall Study App | Transform Notes into Practice Tests
meta.homeDescription: Students waste hours highlighting and rereading, but research shows active recall is 50% more effective. Freshman uses AI to convert your handwritten notes, PDFs, and photos into scientifically-designed active recall questions with spaced repetition for permanent memory retention.
meta.termsDescription: Terms of Use for Freshman, a study app by People Who Code LLC
meta.privacyDescription: Privacy Policy for Freshman, a study app by People Who Code LLC

hero.title: Your Notes Are Useless If You Forget Them By Exam Day.
hero.subtitle: Freshman turns everything you study — notes, textbooks, PDFs, past papers — into AI-powered Active Recall tests that make forgetting nearly impossible. Study less. Retain more. Show up to every exam actually prepared.
hero.supportingLine: No credit card required · Join thousands of students already studying smarter

items.title: Daily Revision
items.text: Freshman generates targeted notes and quick tests for any topic, empowering you to build a daily revision habit that sticks.

items.title: Past Paper Practice
items.text: Upload your old exams and practice them interactively. Turn static past papers into dynamic, auto-graded MCQ tests.

items.title: Comprehensive Testing
items.text: Easily generate quizzes, flashcards, and full mock exams to deeply understand your subjects and instantly identify knowledge gaps.

items.title: Note Breakdown
items.text: Watch your uploaded notes automatically transform into core concepts and targeted revision questions, saving hours of manual synthesis.

dailyFeed.title: revision as simple as swiping
dailyFeed.description: Open Freshman to get a daily stream of bite-sized revision prompts, quick challenges, and concept refreshers tailored to your progress. Keep your streak alive and stay exam-ready with just a few minutes each day.

features.title: Everything You Need. Nothing You Don't.

items.title: Upload Notes, Textbooks & Past Papers
items.description: Your materials become your personalized test bank automatically. No manual card creation. Upload it and Freshman takes it from there.

items.title: Four Distinct Test Modes
items.description: Quizzes. Active Recall. Mock Exams. Past Papers. Each mode serves a different study purpose. Use all four and arrive at exam day having experienced every type of question imaginable.

items.title: Organized Study Library
items.description: All your notes, books, flashcard decks, and subjects — organized and searchable. Browse by subject, search by term, study on demand.

items.title: Share with Your Study Group
items.description: Share notes, flashcard decks, past papers, and textbooks with classmates via a single link. Studying alone is a choice, not a requirement.

items.title: Personalized From Day One
items.description: Dark mode, language preferences, study goals, revision methods — Freshman learns how you study and adapts. Your experience is never one-size-fits-all.

items.title: Track Your Progress
items.description: Session scores, completion streaks, and performance summaries tell you exactly where you're improving and where to focus next. No guessing.

pastPapers.title: practice with real exam questions
pastPapers.description: Upload past papers and instantly transform them into interactive MCQ tests. Practice with real exam questions, receive immediate feedback on each answer, and read detailed explanations to understand every concept. Build confidence by mastering actual exam patterns before test day.

bullets.title: Interactive MCQ Tests
bullets.text: Auto-generated questions from your past papers

bullets.title: Immediate Feedback
bullets.text: Know instantly if your answer is correct or wrong

bullets.title: Detailed Explanations
bullets.text: Understand the reasoning behind every answer

items.question: What makes Freshman different from Anki or Quizlet?
items.answer: Anki and Quizlet require you to build your cards manually. Freshman generates tests automatically from your own uploaded materials, notes, PDFs, textbooks, past papers. You also get a full AI tutor, four test modes, a smart reading workspace, and a daily spaced repetition feed. It's a complete study system, not just a flashcard tool.

items.question: Do I have to create flashcards myself?
items.answer: No, and that's the point. Upload your materials and Freshman builds your tests for you. You focus on studying; we handle the setup.

items.question: What subjects and file types does Freshman support?
items.answer: Freshman works across any subject and supports PDFs, text documents, web URLs, and personal notes. Law, medicine, engineering, humanities, languages if you can upload it, Freshman can test you on it.

items.question: Is there a free plan?
items.answer: Yes. You can start using Freshman for free with access to basic features. Paid plans unlock unlimited usage, the full AI tutor, and all advanced test and exam modes.

items.question: Does it work for postgrads and PhD students, not just undergrads?
items.answer: Absolutely. The web workspace with its source reader, highlight pipeline, and AI tutor is purpose-built for high-volume, reading-heavy study. Postgrads and researchers find it particularly powerful for processing and retaining dense academic material at scale.

items.question: Can I share materials with my study group?
items.answer: Yes. You can share notes, flashcard decks and past papers with classmates via a shareable link. They can open your shared resources directly in their own Freshman app.

items.question: Is my data safe?
items.answer: Your uploaded notes, documents, and study data are stored securely. We don't share your materials or use them to train third-party AI models.

items.question: Is Freshman available on Android?
items.answer: The mobile app is currently available on iOS. The web app runs in any browser on an desktop device. Android app support is planned.

items.question: Can I cancel any time?
items.answer: Always. No cancellation fees, no friction. Cancel directly from your account settings whenever you want.

howItWorks.title: Three Steps Between You and a Better Memory
howItWorks.subtitle: No complicated setup. No starting from scratch. Just upload what you already have and let Freshman do the heavy lifting.

steps.label: STEP 1
steps.title: Upload What You Already Have
steps.description: Notes from lectures. Textbook chapters. PDFs. Past papers. URLs. Drop them into Freshman and your materials are instantly ready to study from.

steps.label: STEP 2
steps.title: Freshman Builds Your Tests Automatically
steps.description: Freshman AI reads your materials and generates perfectly calibrated Active Recall questions — the exact learning method that cognitive scientists have identified as the most effective way to encode information into long-term memory.

steps.label: STEP 3
steps.title: Review Daily. Remember for Good.
steps.description: Your personalized daily feed delivers the right questions at the right time. Swipe through and rate your confidence. Freshman learns how you perform and adjusts so you spend more time on what you do not know and less time on what you have already mastered.

howItWorks.calloutPrefix: Students using spaced Active Recall retain up to

coreFeatures.title: Your Deep Study Command Centre
coreFeatures.subtitle: Stop bouncing between browser tabs, PDF readers, highlighters, and note apps. Freshman brings your reading, annotating, tutoring, and testing into one focused workspace — so nothing falls through the cracks.

features.label: Smart Reader + Highlights
features.title: Read Everything. Highlight What Matters.
features.description: Upload PDFs, paste a URL, or open your own notes directly in the Freshman reader. Highlight key passages as you read — every highlight is saved, searchable, and automatically connected to your study pipeline. Your annotations don't disappear into a folder you'll never open again.

features.label: AI Tutor
features.title: An AI Tutor Who's Actually Read Your Material
features.description: Ask your AI tutor anything — and get answers anchored to your actual source, not generic internet results. Need a concept explained differently? Want to challenge your own understanding? Your tutor knows exactly what you've been reading and meets you where you are.

features.label: Source Library
features.title: All Your Sources. One Searchable Workspace.
features.description: PDFs, textbooks, web articles, personal notes — all organized, retrievable, and ready to study from. Global source search means you're never hunting for that reading you saved three weeks ago.

features.label: Highlight-to-Test Pipeline
features.title: Highlights That Actually Do Something
features.description: Every passage you mark in the reader feeds directly into your tests and revision sessions. This is the pipeline that makes the difference — reading and remembering are no longer two separate activities.

mobileHero.title: The Revision Session That Fits in Your Pocket.
mobileHero.subtitle: While others are cramming the night before, you've been reviewing for weeks — five minutes at a time. Freshman's mobile app turns commutes, lunch breaks, and waiting rooms into daily revision sessions that actually compound. Small habits. Massive results.

dailyFeedHighlight.eyebrow: THE FEATURE STUDENTS COME BACK TO EVERY SINGLE DAY
dailyFeedHighlight.title: Meet Your Daily Revision Feed
paragraphs: Mark each one easy, medium, or hard.
Freshman adjusts daily — more of what you struggle with, less of what you've already locked in.
Spaced repetition and Active Recall, working together, every single day.
stats: No two feeds are the same

additionalFeatures.title: Built for the Way Students Actually Live

items.title: Active Recall, Not Passive Re-reading
items.text: Re-reading your notes feels productive. It isn't. Passive review creates the illusion of knowing until the exam paper lands in front of you. Freshman replaces re-reading with Active Recall: the method that forces retrieval, builds real memory, and makes forgetting structurally harder.

items.title: Consistent Daily Habits Without the Guilt
items.text: You don't need a four-hour study session. You need fifteen focused minutes, every day. Freshman is designed around that reality. Short, satisfying sessions that build into a retention advantage your classmates won't have.

items.title: Arrive at Exam Week Already Prepared
items.text: Because you've been actively recalling your material every day, exam week feels different. Not frantic. Not scrambling. Just a final review of things you already know. That feeling is what Freshman is designed to give you.

items.title: Study in the Language You Think In
items.text: English, Spanish, French, German. Freshman adapts to how you naturally process information. Learning in your strongest language means retaining more, faster.
